name: HHV6_direct_repeat_DR
description: HHV-6A/B direct repeats DRL and DRR (~8 kb each, near-identical) flanking the unique long region. Each DR carries terminal TTAGGG telomeric repeats at its outer end and pac1/pac2 cleavage-packaging signals at the U-junction. The telomeric repeats at the DR termini are the mechanistic substrate for chromosomal integration via homologous recombination with host telomeres — integrants are stable, vertically transmissible, and visible on FISH as a fluorescent signal on a chromosome end. DRL and DRR are >99% identical and require paralog disambiguation when calling integration-junction reads.
expected_copy_count: 2 DR copies per linear genome (left and right)
